nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Abt  .

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from Apt, the Yiddish name of Opatów in Świętokrzyskie Voivodeship, Poland. The placename (in German, Yiddish, and Polish) is from a root meaning ‘abbot’, the place having been named for the local abbey. Compare   Abt  .

variants.name: Abt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Horst, Johann, Kurt.

German and Dutch: from Middle Low German or Middle Dutch abt ‘abbot’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a servant of an abbot or a nickname for someone thought to resemble an abbot in some way. In some instances it may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with this word.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Abt  , a habitational name from Opatów in Poland.

variants.name: Apter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avrohom, Bronya, Eliezer, Naftali.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Apt (see   Apt  ).
English: habitational name from Aptor in Marldon, Devon, composed of Old English uppe ‘up, above’ + torr ‘rocky outcrop’.

nameMeanings.name: Rhys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Welsh:  see  Rees  .

variants.name: Rees
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Rice  .
German: habitational name from a place in the Rhineland called Rees.
German: from a short form of the personal name   Andreas  .

nameMeanings.name: Wendot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Kurt, Erwin, Hans, Dieter, Heinz, Reinhold, Wolfgang, Armin, Erhard, Ernst.

German and Danish: ethnic name for a Wend, Middle High German wind(e). The Wends (i.e. Polabian Slavs, Pomeranians, and Sorbians) once occupied a large area of northeastern and eastern Germany (extending as far west as Lüneburg, with an area called Wendland; see Wendland ), and many German placenames and surnames are of Wendish origin. Today, of all Wendish people only Sorbians still exist (in Lusatia in eastern Germany). See also Serbin 2.

variants.name: Windt
meanings: German: variant of   Wendt  .
Dutch: variant of   Wind  .

variants.name: Wende
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Hans, Juergen.
German: variant of   Wendt  .

variants.name: Serber
meanings: German: ethnic name for a Sorb (a member of the Slavic people in Lusatia in eastern Germany; see Wendt ), from an inflected form of the adjective Serbe, derived from the Sorbian ethnonym Serb (see Serbin ). This surname is very rare in Germany. Compare Server 1.

variants.name: Wuensch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Erna, Siegfried.
German (Wünsch): ethnic name for a Wend (see Wendt ). Compare Wunsch 2.

variants.name: Wendland
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Volker, Armin, Ernst, Erwin, Gunter, Gunther, Heinz, Juergen, Manfred, Reinhard, Wolfgang.

German: habitational name from the area of northeastern Germany inhabited by the Wends (see   Wendt  ), especially the Wendland district near Lüneburg, where Wendish continued to be spoken as late as the 18th century. Compare   Windland  .

variants.name: Windisch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Lutz, Florian, Horst, Manfred, Otto.

German: ethnic name from Middle High German windisch ‘Wendish, Slavic’, in Austria more narrowly ‘Slovenian’, in Slovakia ‘Slovak’. The name was also applied as a nickname for someone who had connections with Slavic areas. See also Wendt , compare Windish .
In some cases possibly also a Germanized form of Slovak and Slovenian Vindiš: ethnic name for a Slovak or Slovene, of the same German origin as 1 above.

variants.name: Wend
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans.
German: variant of   Wendt  .

nameMeanings.name: Goch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Italian Rocco; .

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Maredudd Goch ap Rhys Wendot was born about 1326, in Lleyn, Caernarfonshire, Wales. She had at least 2 sons with Maredydd " Goch" ap Rhys " Wendot".

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Maredudd Goch ap Rhys Wendot est née environ 1326, à Lleyn, Caernarfonshire, Galles. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Maredydd " Goch" ap Rhys " Wendot".

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Maredudd Goch ap Rhys Wendot wurde um 1326 in Lleyn, Caernarfonshire, Wales geboren. Sie hatte mit Maredydd " Goch" ap Rhys " Wendot" mindestens 2 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Maredudd Goch ap Rhys Wendot è nata il circa 1326 nel Lleyn, Caernarfonshire, Galles. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Maredydd " Goch" ap Rhys " Wendot".

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Maredudd Goch ap Rhys Wendot(은)는 대략 1326년 웨일스 카이른아르폰실르 린에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Maredydd " Goch" ap Rhys " Wendot"(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. Maredudd Goch ap Rhys Wendot nasceu aproximadamente 1326, em Lleyn, Caernarfonshire, Gales. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Maredydd " Goch" ap Rhys " Wendot".

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Maredudd Goch ap Rhys Wendot родилась около 1326 г. в местности ллин, каирнарфоншир, Уэльс. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Maredydd " Goch" ap Rhys " Wendot".

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. Maredudd Goch ap Rhys Wendot nació aproximadamente 1326, en Lleyn, Caernarfonshire, Gales. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Maredydd " Goch" ap Rhys " Wendot".
